title: Golden Thai Chickpeas Khanom Krok
description: Rich, crumbly, and indulgent Thai dish featuring chickpeas in a corn husk wrap, perfect for a snack or dessert. This macrobiotic-friendly recipe is easy to make and delicious to eat.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a blender, combine chickpeas, coconut milk, palm sugar, rice flour, salt, and paprika, and blend until smooth.
Lay a corn husk flat and spoon about 1 tablespoon of the chickpea mixture onto the center of the husk.
Fold the sides of the husk over the filling and then roll the husk up to form a tight cylinder.
Repeat with the remaining corn husks and filling.
Place the khanom krok in a single layer on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Drizzle the tops with vegetable oil and bake for 20 minutes.
Remove the khanom krok from the oven and let cool for 5 minutes before serving.
